mnemonics: BISC-LOFT: Byte, Int, Short, Char, Long - Order From Tiny (to large capacity, signed/unsigned details)
keyPoints: Java has 4 signed integer types: byte (8-bit), short (16-bit), int (32-bit), long (64-bit)
char is a 16-bit unsigned integer representing Unicode characters
Default integer type is int; use L suffix for long literals (e.g., 12345678901L)
commonMistakes: Using int for calculations that exceed 2^31 (causing overflow)
Forgetting L suffix on long literals (causing compiler errors)
Treating char as signed integer (it's unsigned 0-65535)
